payload.answer: Saved minimal memory about the failed fetch and the user-stated intended usefulness; did not store any claim about the article’s contents.

entity_resolution.reason: No stable entities beyond the provided URL and the concept were verifiable from fetch failure; do not create additional IDs.

payload.intent: Store a memory card about an attempted article fetch and its relevance (memory architecture), without recording unverified article contents.

memory_cards.statement: An attempt to fetch https://example.com/missing resulted in a 404 (network error). The article content could not be retrieved.
The user indicated the (unretrieved) article would be useful for memory architecture, but no article details were successfully fetched.

details: Stored fetch failure status for the provided URL.
Stored a note that the user intended the (unretrieved) content to be useful for memory architecture.

repair_options: Provide the article text or a working URL.
Retry fetch at a later time or using an alternative source (mirror, cached version).
Supply key excerpts so the memory system can store verified content.
